quests.title: Grow Your Quartz
quests.subtitle: AE2 starts in a bucket of water.
description: Applied Energistics 2 requires Certus Quartz — and the best source is growing it yourself.
Throw Certus Quartz Seeds into water. They grow over time into full crystals.
Fluix Crystals are made by throwing Certus Quartz, Nether Quartz, and Redstone into water simultaneously.
Both are essential. Grow a solid supply before moving on — you'll go through them fast.

quests.title: ME Controller
quests.subtitle: The brain of your network. Treat it well.
description: The ME Controller is the central node of every AE2 network.
It requires a constant supply of power (FE) to operate — connect it to your energy grid.
Important: The recipe has been modified. It requires Precision Mechanisms as a core component.
This enforces the Create Tier 2 progression — no shortcuts to AE2.
Place it, power it, and watch it glow blue. You're in the future now.

quests.title: Your First ME Network
quests.subtitle: Storage accessible from anywhere. Yes, anywhere.
description: An ME Drive holds Storage Cells — your digital inventory.
An ME Terminal gives you access to everything stored in the network from a single interface.
Connect Controller → Drive → Terminal using ME Cables (glass or smart).
Once your network is live, you'll wonder how you ever played without it.
Tip: Start with 4k or 16k Storage Cells. Upgrade as your item count grows.

quests.title: Autocrafting
quests.subtitle: Click a button. Get the thing. Magic.
description: AE2 Autocrafting lets the network craft any item for you, automatically, on demand.
The Molecular Assembler performs the actual crafting. The Pattern Provider feeds it patterns.
Encode patterns using the Pattern Encoding Terminal: define what goes in, what comes out.
Set up crafting patterns for your most-used items (iron ingots, circuits, casings) and never craft manually again.
This is the most powerful system in the modpack. Invest in it early, expand it often.

quests.title: Rotation Becomes Power
quests.subtitle: Create spins things. Mekanism wants electricity. Problem solved.
description: Create generates Rotational Force (SU/RPM). Mekanism and AE2 need Forge Energy (FE). These are not the same.
Create: New Age bridges this gap. The Alternator converts rotational force into FE.
The more RPM you feed it, the more FE/t it produces. A large water wheel setup can power your early Mekanism machines comfortably.
Connect the Alternator's output to an Energy Cube or directly to machines via XNet cables.

quests.title: Energy Storage
quests.subtitle: Buffer your power. Machines are needy.
description: Generators produce energy in bursts. Machines demand a constant supply. An Energy Cube smooths this out.
The Basic Energy Cube stores 4M FE. Connect your Alternator to it and your machines to the other side.
Also craft a Basic Solar Generator — it produces free FE during daylight. No fuel required, no maintenance needed.
Passive solar + Create Alternator gives you a solid, self-sustaining early power setup.

quests.title: XNet — The Nervous System
quests.subtitle: One cable to rule them all.
description: XNet cables are the most efficient multi-purpose cable in the pack.
A single XNet network can simultaneously handle: items, fluids, energy, and redstone signals.
Build an XNet Controller, add Connectors to machines, and configure each connection in the Controller GUI.
This replaces a mess of separate item pipes, fluid pipes, and energy cables with a single, clean network.
Essential for connecting your ore chain, energy grid, and storage system in one coherent setup.

quests.title: Air Compressor
quests.subtitle: PneumaticCraft: compressed air is also energy, apparently.
description: PneumaticCraft uses compressed air (mBar) as its energy source — separate from FE entirely.
The Air Compressor generates pressure by burning fuel. The higher the pressure, the faster your machines work.
Recipe note: The Air Compressor recipe has been modified to require Create components — enforcing Brass-tier progression.
Connect the compressor to a Pressure Gauge and watch the needle climb. Don't let it explode.

quests.title: Pressure Chamber
quests.subtitle: A cube of walls that does things under pressure. Literally.
description: The Pressure Chamber is the core crafting station of PneumaticCraft.
Build a hollow 3x3x3 cube using Pressure Chamber Walls and two Pressure Chamber Interfaces (for item I/O).
Fill it with compressed air from your compressor and insert items. Some recipes produce components only obtainable here.
Compressed Iron — the Nether Star of PneumaticCraft — comes from here. You'll need a lot of it.

quests.title: Mystical Agriculture: Beginnings
quests.subtitle: You can grow iron now. Wrap your head around that.
description: Mystical Agriculture lets you farm resources the same way you'd farm wheat — except instead of bread, you get diamonds.
Inferium Essence is the foundation of everything. Every higher-tier seed requires it.
Plant Inferium Seeds on regular Farmland. Harvest the essence and use it to upgrade to higher tiers.
A Watering Can or automated harvesting (Create) dramatically speeds up the process.

quests.title: Tier 2 Seeds
quests.subtitle: Iron. From dirt. We don't make the rules.
description: Tier 2 seeds produce Iron, Coal, Stone, and Wood Essences — the basics, grown sustainably.
To craft them you need Prudentium Essence, which is crafted from Inferium Essence.
Once you have Iron Seeds running, your ore dependency for basic metals starts to fade.
Automate the harvest with Create (Mechanical Harvester on a Contraption) for maximum throughput.

quests.title: Tier 3 Complete
quests.subtitle: AE2, power, pressure, crops — Tier 4 opens up.
description: AE2 is running, your energy grid is stable, PneumaticCraft is pressurized, and crops are growing.
Tier 4 introduces 3x ore processing, MEGA Storage Cells, and the Resource Catalyst system — the gate to Mystical Agriculture Tier 3.
Engineering Processors are the backbone of all AE2 components. Make extras.
Advanced Control Circuits unlock Mekanism Tier 3 machines. You'll need both going forward.
